text: now we come to the book of the 11th 11.6 the buddha said monks if any monk abuses and reviles rails at the aryans who are who are his fellows in the holy life it is utterly impossible it is unavoidable that he should not come to one or other of 11 disasters what eleven he fails to attain the unattained two from one he has attained he falls away three true dhamma is not made clear for him four he is conceited about true dharmas five he follows the holy life without delight six he commits some foul offense seven he gives up the training and falls back to the low life that means the life as a layman eight he falls into some grievous sickness nine he goes out of his mind with distraction then he makes an end with mind confused that means he dies with mind confused eleven when body breaks up beyond death he rises up again in the ways the ill-born the downfall in hell monks if any man abuses and reviles rails at the aryans who are his fellows in the holy life it is utterly impossible it is unavoidable that he should not come to one or other of these 11 disasters so here that's the end of the sutra so here the buddha is saying if we abuse a holy man and aryan here is talking about monks like if monks abuse other holy monks then they can suffer one of these 11 um disasters number one he fails to attain the unattained that means what uh holy stage yeah aryan stage he has not attained you will feel to get it now number two what he ascertained he falls away for example uh if there's attain concentration and then he gets angry with some some holy monks then he might lose his concentration just like devadatta devadatta at one time he attained all the eight janas so that he had psychic power but later because he wanted to take over the buddha's place as leader of the monks and the buddha did not allow him so he schemed to kill the buddha and he talked bad about the buddha so all the psychic power that he had attained all fell away he lost all his psychic power number three true dhamma is not made clear for him he is not able to see the true dharma or he is conceited about true dharmas he is swollen hated because he knows a bit of dhamma number five he does not delight in the holy life that means he wears the rope he's not happy on number six he commits some foul offense he breaks some some precepts some heavy precepts on number seven he gives up the training as a monk and he goes back to the lay line number eight he becomes sick grievously sick just like devadatta after trying to kill the buddha later he became grievously ill and then he felt so felt sorry for what he did and then when he wanted to come and apologize to the buddha it was too late it's too late he died before he could see the buddha then number nine he might go out of his mind with distraction that means his mind is very restless very scattered because of talking bad about holy man so he's very uh his mind is very scattered number 10 him he dies with mind confused when he's about to die his mind is very confused then number 11 after his dinah he might be reborn in hell because of reviling holy men [Music] now suta 11.7 remember ananda came to see the exalted one paid homage to exalted one and sat down to one side then he asked the buddha he said please sir may it be that amongst winning of concentration is of such a sort that in earth he is unperceptive of earth in water unperceptive of water in fire unperceptive of fire in air unperceptive of air in the realm of unbounded space unperceptive of it in the realm of unbounded consciousness unperceptive of it in the realm of nothingness and perceptive of it in the realm of neither perception or non-perception also aren't perceptive of it that in this world he is unaware of this unperceptive of this world in the world beyond his unperceptive of the world beyond that whatsoever is seen heard sensed cognized attained sought after taught over by mind of all that he is unperceptive and yet he perceives and the buddha said it may be so ananda amongst meaning of concentration may be of such a sort that in earth he is unperceptive of earth etc etc then remember ananda asked but sir in what way may amongst winning of concentration be of such a sort that of all that he is unperceptive and yet he is perceptive he perceives and then the buddha said here in ananda among his conscious dust this is the real this is the best namely the calming of all conditioners the rejection of every substrate the ending of craving the fading of interest stopping and cessation and ibana in such way ananda amongst winning of concentration may be of such a thought that in earth he is unperceptive of earth etc that in whatsoever is seen hurt sense cognized attained sought after thought out by mind of all that he is unperceptive and yet at the same time he perceives and that's the end of the sutta it's quite an interesting suta here the member ananda is asking can a manga attain a state of concentration such that no [Music] he is in this world and yet no he is not perceptive of this world usually [Music] the buddha talks about feeling and perception if a person has feeling and perception it means he has consciousness he is conscious so if a person uh has no perception now it also means he has no consciousness but this in this case the buddha is talking about a person who has attained a state of concentration that he is not aware of this world at all he's not aware of he's not aware of whatever is seen sense heard cognized thought over etc and yet now at the same time he is conscious so the buddha said it is possible it is possible and he can attain this stage when he calms down all his conditioners and he has he's is disenchanted with everything in this world and he attains cessation and here he even says nibana [Music] so here basically what is being said now is that this state of nibana is of this sort if a person attains nibana a sixth consciousness will stop working this consciousness will stop working and yet there appears to be another type of consciousness another type of consciousness where that person is not even aware of himself so sometimes some monks say when a person attains nibana there is totally no consciousness and no any type not any type of consciousness at all but in certain sutas like in the vada sutta in the diga nikaya it is mentioned that in the state of nibana consciousness is boundless and luminous all brighter and yet in that state now there is no small no big no nama no rupa in other words when you have nama rupa you must have consciousness vinyana vinyana always comes with nama rupa because nama rupa is the object of consciousness mentality and materiality is always the object of consciousness so if you don't have nama rupa it means that you don't have consciousness but yet the buddha says there is a type of consciousness which is luminous and which is boundless so probably here is also referring to the same thing now we come to the last 11.16 the buddha said monks 11 advantages are to be looked for from the radiation by mind of loving-kindness by making a loving kindness grow by making much of it by making loving kindness a vehicle and basis by persisting in it by becoming familiar with it by well establishing it what are the eleven one sleeps happy and wakes happy he dreams no evil dream he is dear to human beings and non-human beings are like the devas or heavenly beings guard him fire poison or sword affect him not quickly he concentrates his mind his complexion is serene he makes an end or he dies without bewilderment it means unconfused and if he has penetrated no further he reaches upon dying the brahma world these 11 advantages are to be looked for from the radiation by mind of loving kindness by making loving-kindness grow by making much of it by making loving-kindness a vehicle and basis by persisting in it by becoming familiar with it by well establishing it that's the end of the suta [Music] here i have translated this radiation by mind of loving kindness here this in the polytech society book they have it as the release of heart by the practice of enmity release i uh it's like liberation liberation here i prefer the word radiation are you radiate loving kindness by the mind in the sutas the buddha has said now that to practice loving kindness we should first attain the jana at least the first jana because if a person has attained jhana the mind is strong then when he practices radiating out the loving kindness it is possible but if we don't have a strong mind we try to radiate the loving kindness other beings cannot feel it so if a person can practice this radiation by mind of loving kindness it means he has attained the jhanas and if a person has attained the jhanas so he gets these benefits by practicing methala he sleeps happy and wakes happy he dreams no evil dream he is dear to human beings and non-human beings alike because he's always having meta the devas guard him fire poison or sword affect him not this one is because the devas guard him sometimes people think even if he takes poison he won't die but it's not he i don't think it's in that sense la there is a sense that because he is protected by devasa then even if he were to maybe take some some poison or anything or or fire or sword anything might want to harm him but the devas will protect him and then he can concentrate his mind quickly his complexion is very serene and he dies unconfused and he has if he has not attained to arahan hudna he will attain to the brahma world brahma world is in the form [Music] realm rupa loka and in the form realm generally you should have jhana to be able to be reborn then so these are the advantages of practicing meta loving-kindness so with that now i come to the end of the angutra nikaya
